chapter_function: Front matter establishes the book's central thesis that the hotel functions as a microcosm reflecting Parisian society during WWII, introduces all major historical figures who will appear across subsequent chapters, and sets the geographic/temporal/spatial parameters of the narrative scope while establishing authorial voice and source methodology.

state_changes.after: Author declares personal discovery process and poses central questions
state_changes.story_value: Establishes investigative credibility and frames reader expectations

reader_rewards: Clear understanding of the hotel's role as narrative/structural device
Familiarity with ~40 named characters and their affiliations
Geographic grounding in the Place Vendome and hotel layout
Temporal boundaries: 1940-1945 with pre-war context
Thesis statement: hotel operations shaped EU formation through economic negotiations
List of chapter topics indicating full historical arc from occupation to liberation
setups: Central thesis about hotel reflecting Parisian society
Multiple resistance and collaboration storylines introduced
German high command figures established as recurring presence
American correspondents and military figures positioned for later appearances

Vichy political figures established for moral complexity arc
The瓦尔基里 assassination plot mentioned as throughline

What specific secrets did the Swiss manager and his wife harbor?

reusable_mechanisms: Anchoring narrative scope to a single geographic location
Using hospitality spaces as stages for political/economic negotiation
Interweaving celebrity figures with historical events for reader accessibility
Presenting collaboration and resistance as coexisting moral spectrums
Using character lists as chapter preview mechanism
Establishing authorial discovery process to frame historical investigation

craft_observations.pov_distance: Epistemic distance varies between sections—epigraph uses literary third person, authorial voice shifts to investigative first person in preface, character list uses encyclopedic distance
craft_observations.sentence_rhythm: Preface employs longer, reflective periods establishing gravitas; character entries use compact declarative sentences for quick identification
craft_observations.paragraphing: Character list entries are consistently single-paragraph, allowing rapid scanning; preface uses flowing multi-sentence paragraphs for thematic development
craft_observations.dialogue_method: No dialogue in front matter; all information conveyed through declarative exposition or character summary
craft_observations.description_method: Character descriptions emphasize single distinguishing trait per entry for memorability; hotel description uses sensory and social details to establish atmosphere
craft_observations.exposition_method: Thesis delivered through direct assertion, qualified by literary epigraph; historical scope established through chapter listing rather than explicit summary
craft_observations.hook_delivery: Opening hooks use personal discovery narrative and rhetorical questions to create intrigue before historical scope
craft_observations.transition_method: Moves from copyright formality through catalog (contents, characters) to essay (preface), creating escalating intimacy
do_not_copy: Do not imitate the literary epigraph attribution as chapter opening
Do not reproduce the character entry format verbatim
Do not copy the source-specific phrase patterns

non_reusable_specifics: All character names: Hemingway, Chanel, Goering, Dietrich, etc.
Hotel name and Place Vendome location
Specific WWII events and dates listed
Vichy government figures and policies
Intelligence operation details
Literary and artistic historical figures' biographies
EU formation negotiation details
risk_flags: Historical nonfiction—may contain verification gaps in character anecdotes
Collaborator sympathetic portrayal risks reader moral confusion
Celebrity historical figures may overshadow documented historical events
Authorial first-person framing may introduce subjectivity bias
Character list format may create false expectations of novelistic treatment

Profile American socialite presence at the Ritz during occupation
Examine moral ambiguity and survival strategies of wealthy expatriates

state_changes.before: Pre-war Parisian luxury and social prominence
state_changes.after: Nazi occupation and occupation-era survival economics

state_changes.before: Ritz as symbol of pre-war European glamour
state_changes.after: Ritz as contested space between occupation forces and resistance networks

state_changes.after: firsthand witness accounts and tribunal evidence establish facts about Nazi capabilities and atrocities

state_changes.before: characters held compartmentalized views of wartime allegiances
state_changes.after: simultaneous exposure to nuclear stakes and Holocaust evidence forces integrated moral reckoning

state_changes.before: certain figures operated under cloud of suspicion or exile

state_changes.after: letters and testimony create documentary record of collaboration and resistance

setups: nuclear physicist's earlier expressed willingness to consider catastrophic options
hotel suite converted to tribunal setting establishes improvisational justice

payoffs: soldier's account is reinterpreted through postwar lens of collaboration investigation

testimonial frame—firsthand accounts quoted to provide experiential immediacy
legal/political resolution scene—official decisions announced that crystallize character fates
retrospective reinterpretation—earlier events recontextualized by new documentary evidence
fictional intermediary—real historical figures processed through scenes that function as interpretive proxies

craft_observations.pov_distance: omniscient narrator with documentary access; citations anchor claims while scenes dramatize documented exchanges
craft_observations.sentence_rhythm: citation-heavy passages alternate with kinetic scene dialogue; short declarative sentences dominate testimony, complex subordinate clauses handle contextual layering
craft_observations.paragraphing: each numbered note functions as discrete paragraph; scene passages use block dialogue with attribution tags
craft_observations.dialogue_method: quoted exchanges from letters, interviews, and imagined reconstructions labeled with documentary sources
craft_observations.description_method: location and atmosphere subordinated to evidentiary detail; sensory specificity deployed in testimony passages only
craft_observations.exposition_method: inline citations serve dual purpose of attribution and worldbuilding; no separate information dumps
craft_observations.hook_delivery: chapter openings plunge into specific quoted statement or documented exchange; no transitional framing
craft_observations.transition_method: thematic parallel (investigation of German program / investigation of German conduct) provides connective tissue; physical location shifts handled through returning residents
